verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family of sperm-egg fusion proteins. While IZUMO1 is the canonical sperm-surface fusogen, IZUMO4 is expressed in testis/sperm and is thought to associate with IZUMO1 in a complex on the sperm surface. It has a signal peptide and is reported to be largely secreted/soluble, but it is displayed on sperm in the context of the IZUMO complex. Surface presence is restricted to the germline lineage (sperm), making this tissue-restricted surface display rather than broad surface accessibility. Not cytoplasmic/nuclear/ER-resident only; not pMHC-only; not lysosomal exocytosis; cancer/cell-death ecto-display not documented; covalent TM-partner tethering not established — best fit is narrow germline-restricted surface display via complex association.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Unclear whether IZUMO4 stably resides on the sperm surface via complex with IZUMO1 or is predominantly secreted/soluble; could alternatively be stable_complex_partner or secreted_only.
